title: hoya heuschkeliana subsp. cajanoae
intro: Hoya heuschkeliana subsp. cajanoae is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the waxflower or hoya. It is a climbing plant native to the Philippines, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant's leaves and roots are used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
